canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, "Knowledge utilization" is most commonly defined as the degree to which a model accesses and applies its inherent, pre-trained knowledge during task performance. Alternative framings also appear, with some work defining it as the capacity to apply newly learned facts, and others focusing specifically on specialized, domain-specific knowledge. A distinct usage, sometimes co-occurring with the pre-trained knowledge definition, treats it as the ability to leverage information provided within the input prompt or retrieved context. The construct is operationalized through specific benchmarks; for example, the DDXPlus dataset is used to assess the application of expert knowledge. Research aims to "strengthen their utilization of knowledge autonomously" (AlphaMath Almost Zero), though one study notes this ability can be "unstable and even counterintuitive" (Delving into the Reversal Curse). Another paper suggests that in-context learning "enhances the... knowledge utilization capabilities" of models (AISees YourLocation).
papers: AISees YourLocation—But With A Bias Toward The Wealthy World
Understanding and Leveraging the Expert Specialization of Context Faithfulness in Mixture-of-ExpertsLLMs
AlphaMath Almost Zero: Process Supervision without Process
Delving into the Reversal Curse: How Far Can Large Language Models Generalize?
StreamBench: Towards Benchmarking Continuous Improvement of Language Agents

definitions.text: The degree to which a model effectively accesses and applies its inherent pre-trained knowledge during task performance.
definitions.paper: AlphaMath Almost Zero: Process Supervision without Process

definitions.text: The capacity to use newly learned factual knowledge to answer downstream questions correctly, especially when the query format differs from the training statement.
definitions.paper: Delving into the Reversal Curse: How Far Can Large Language Models Generalize?

definitions.text: The ability of a model to apply specialized, domain-specific knowledge, such as in making medical diagnoses.
definitions.paper: StreamBench: Towards Benchmarking Continuous Improvement of Language Agents

relationships.paper: StreamBench: Towards Benchmarking Continuous Improvement of Language Agents
